rulings.Case Title: CESAR ASCARRUNZ VS. HOWARD HSU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 11, 2013, Line 1, DEFENDANT GRANT KWOK's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants' Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to all causes of action. Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice is granted. Per the August 2, 2013 Order of this Court, the Second Amended Complaint does not relate back to the original complaint because additional and different facts were alleged. The SAC was filed on July 28, 2011, after the Statute of Limitations had run for all causes of action. (See CCP §§ 337 - 338.) Plaintiff's position that the demurrer should be overruled because it was calendared 38 days after the filing of the demurrer is rejected. CRC § 3.1320(d) states that "demurrers must be set for hearing not more than 35 days following the filing of the demurrer or on the first date available to the court thereafter." Plaintiff has produced no evidence that the demurrer was not scheduled on the first date available to the court. Additionally, even if the hearing was set 38 days out in error, this error is de minimus. Plaintiff does not argue that this error materially prejudiced him in any way. Further, it is a general legal principle that deciding cases on substantive issues rather than procedural grounds is preferred, especially when the procedural issue is de minimus. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/LCN)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK CAFFERKEY et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Preventing Pltf From Taking The Deposition Of Deputy City
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 11, 2013, Line 1: Defendant CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S (Amended) Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Preventing Plaintiff From Taking The Deposition Of Deputy City Attorney Carole F. Ruwart Part 1 of 2: Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Judge Pro-Tem Ernst Halperin discloses that in 2011 he represented a client in litigation in Sonoma County Superior Court and Plaintiff's counsel Jeffrey H. Lowenthal represented the opposing party. He does not believe this fact requires recusal under California Code of Judicial Ethics Cannon 6(D)(3), but encourages the parties to raise any concerns they may have regarding this disclosure prior to the hearing. Defendant's motion is DENIED IN PART and GRANTED IN PART. Plaintiffs Frank and Maureen Cafferkey have met their burden of establishing good cause to take the deposition of Deputy City Attorney Carole F. Ruwart. Plaintiffs assert that they desire to take the deposition of Attorney Ruwart to obtain evidence of statements she made to the Assessment Appeals Board during a September 12, 2012 hearing. Plaintiffs maintain that these statements constitute a concession that the City created and taxed APN 4329-18 by mistake. According to evidence submitted by the City, the Board does not provide written transcripts of its proceedings, but will provide participants with a taped recording. The City's reply brief represents that the City has objected to the admissibility of transcripts of the September 12, 2012 Board hearing that Plaintiffs submitted to the Court in connection with Plaintiffs' motion for summary judgment. On these facts, Plaintiffs have established good cause to take Attorney Ruwart's deposition in order to develop admissible evidence for trial concerning statements she made to the Board on September 12, 2013. Plaintiffs' questioning shall be limited to the following topics: (1) what Attorney Ruwart said during the September 12, 2012 Assessment Appeals Board hearing, and (2) whether Plaintiffs' transcripts of the oral proceedings accurately reflect what was said by Assessment Appeals Board members and Deputy City Attorney Ruwart during the September 12, 2012 Assessment Appeals Board hearing. [Continued to Part 2 of 2] =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ALL ENTHUSIAST INC. DBA DEALIGHTED.COM VS. NEXTAG INC., DBA NEXTAG.COM, A CALIFORNIA COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Production Of Discovery Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 11, 2013, Line 6: Plaintiff ALL ENTHUSIAST INC. DBA DEALIGHTED.COM'S Motion To Compel Production Of Discovery Documents Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Judge Pro Tem Halperin discloses that a former college roommate of his is a partner with a transactional practice at Cadwalader Wickersham & Taft. He does not believe this fact requires recusal under the California Code of Judicial Ethics Cannon 6(D)(3), but encourages the parties to raise any concerns they may have regarding this disclosure prior to the hearing. Plaintiff All Enthusiast Inc.'s motion to compel is GRANTED. Plaintiff's motion is timely in that it is a motion to compel compliance with Defendant Nextag Inc.'s responses to Plaintiff's Demand for Production, Set One. Such a motion is governed by Code of Civil Procedure Section 2031.320 and is not subject to the 45-day deadline for serving notice of a motion to compel further responses under Code of Civil procedure Section 2031.310(c). Within 30 days Defendant Nextag Inc. shall produce all of the electronically stored information concerning data computations identified by Mr. Aoyama on pages 42-43, 55-58, 61, 63, 68, 91, 96, 113, 123, 125, 135, 150, 154, 161, 183-186, 194, 220-221, 226, 240-241, 243 & 250 of the transcript of his August 12, 2013 deposition. Both parties' requests for sanctions are DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN COWSILL et al VS. UMG RECORDING, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Andmotion For An Order Requiring The Return Of Privileged And Inadvertently-Disclosed Documents And Amendment Of Deposition Exhibit;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 11, 2013, Line 3: Defendant UMG RECORDING, INC.'S Motion For An Order Requiring The Return Of Privileged And Inadvertently-Disclosed Documents And Amendment Of Deposition Exhibit Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED. Exhibit 4 to the Declaration of Sheryl Gold submitted in connection with Defendant UMG Recording, Inc.'s reply brief establishes that Polygram Records, Inc. and Defendant UMG Recording, inc. are one and the same company. Therefore, UMG Recording, Inc. may assert work-product and attorney-client privilege in the memorandum from Polygram lawyer Janna Glasser to Polygram attorney Lisa Rothblum. Defendant's counsel's inadvertent production of the document in the context of a deposition that she noticed does not make the disclosure "without coercion" within the meaning of Evidence Code Section 912. See Rico v. Mitsubishi Motors Corp. (2007) 42 Cal.4th 807 (affirming disqualification of plaintiff's counsel and plaintiff's experts when plaintiff's counsel came into possession of Defendant's counsel's work-product at a deposition noticed by Defendant's counsel). Paragraphs 8 through 13 of the declaration of Plaintiffs' counsel Steven Ames Brown do not establish that PolyGram waived any privilege in the Glasser memorandum during the Seagram acquisition, given that the declaration only discusses Mr. Brown's experience with other companies and provides no evidence concerning the Seagram acquistion itself. Within 5 days of this order Plaintiffs shall return to defendant UMG Recordings Inc. any and all copies of the Glasser memorandum in their possession, custody or control. The deposition exhibit to which the Glasser memorandum was attached is ordered amended so that the Glasser memorandum is removed. Request for sanctions is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. The trial date for this matter has previously been continued from October 21, 2013 to March 24, 2014. The parties shall meet and confer prior to the hearing to agree on a date within the next 45 days for the deposition of Plaintiff URS Corporation's person most knowledgeable and custodian of records, and shall report that date to the court at the hearing. The court will enter an order at the hearing that the deposition and document production shall take place on the date and time chosen by the parties. Request for sanctions is DENIED. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: VERNON S. HANEY JR. VS. ALI AZHAR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 11, 2013, Line 12, DEFENDANT ALI AZHAR's AMENDED MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. The Motion to Set Aside Default is granted. Defendant is entitled to relief under Cal. Civ. Proc. § 473(b) because the default was entered due to Defendant's insurance company's mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect. Defendant may file his proposed answer within 10 days. Defendant is ordered to pay reasonable attorney's fees and costs to Plaintiff pursuant to CCP § 473(b) in the amount of $585.00. rulings.Case Title: THE BECKERMAN GROUP, INC.DBA BECKERMAN, A NEW VS. CAROLINE VENZA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 11, 2013, Line 15, DEFENDANTs CAROLINE VENZA & MISSION CTRL COMMUNICATIONS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to causes of action 2 through 4. The only actionable conduct that plaintiff cites parrots the misappropriation cause of action. In other words, the common law and UCL causes of action are based on the same nucleus of facts as the misappropriation claim. Even if defendant's pre-resignation conduct is distinct from the misappropriation, plaintiff cannot rely on that conduct under the parties' release, which is attached to the AC. Plaintiff's interpretation of the separation agreement (that the fiduciary duty survived the resignation) is unreasonable. Such an interpretation would run afoul of B&P 16600. Defendant owed a continuing duty to protect plaintiff's trade secrets. Defendant does not owe a continuing duty to act as plaintiff's fiduciary. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/LCN)
